expectations.text: Identifies the key decision: start with modular monolith, extract later
expectations.evidence: D-001 explicitly states 'Adopt modular monolith over microservices as the initial architecture' and D-002 captures the extraction criteria: 'Extract services only when a team proves it needs deployment independence'. The closing note also confirms: 'The user came in with microservices but landed on modular monolith — that's a genuine decision.'

expectations.text: Captures the reasoning: team autonomy is the driver
expectations.evidence: D-003 is entirely dedicated to this: 'Team autonomy is the primary architectural driver, not scale or deployment speed'. Context notes 'The user confirmed autonomy as the driver when prompted.'

expectations.text: Assigns appropriate domain tags like architecture or technical
expectations.evidence: All three decisions include 'architecture' as a domain tag. Additional relevant tags are assigned: 'system-design', 'team-structure', and 'org-design'. These are appropriate and specific.

expectations.text: Suggests a confidence level based on the conviction shown
expectations.evidence: D-001: 'high — both parties aligned after pushback'. D-002: 'high — explicit agreement, clear trigger condition'. D-003: 'medium — stated as the main motivation, but scale/deployment weren't deeply explored. Could resurface.' The medium confidence on D-003 is well-reasoned since autonomy was stated but not deeply stress-tested.

expectations.text: Does not extract the initial 'split into microservices' as a decision since it was revised
expectations.evidence: No decision captures 'split into microservices' as a standalone conclusion. D-001 explicitly frames microservices as the rejected option: 'Microservices was the initial impulse, but modular monolith was accepted as the pragmatic starting point after challenging the assumption.' The closing note reinforces this: 'the strongest signal here is the pivot.'
